hadiths.chapter: 21. THE CHAPTER ON TAYAMMUM (DRY ABLUTION)
hadiths.ar:
222   -  وَّ إ َّي َّى إ َّن ُّو ذ َّرٍّ رَّجِمَّهُّ إَّلل َّهُّ إَّلن  َّن ِى  َّ ضَّ ق َّق َّال هَّلَّكْث ُّ ج َّامَّعْث ُّ عَّلَّى ع َّي ْزِ مَّاءٍ ق َّالَّ ق َّا َّمَّزَّ إَّلن  َّن ِى  ُّ صَّل َّى  
إَّلل َّهُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ وَّ إ لِهِ ن ِمَّحْمِلٍ ق َّاسْي َّي َّزْن َّا ن ِهِ وَّ ن ِمَّاءٍ ق َّاع ْي َّسَّلْث ُّ إ َّن َّا وَّ هِى َّ ي ُّم َّ ق َّال   ي َّكْق ِي ك َّ إَّلص َّعِي ذُّ عَّس ْزَّ سِن ِي ن  
وإ ذ إ إ ج ن ث  إلزج ل ق ى  سق ز ومعه ماء ق ذر ما ي ي وص ا  ن ه ي ي مم ولم ي ي وص ا  إ لأ إ ن  ي علم   إ ن ه ي ذرك  إلماء ق ن ل إ ن  ي ق وي ه وق ث  
إلصلاة .
hadiths.en:
Mu'awiyah ibn Maythara asked Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al -Sadiq {a.s} about 
a man who is traveling, does not find water, performs tayammum, and prays. Then he 
finds water while there is still some time left (for the prayer). Should he continue with 
his prayer or perform wudu and repeat the prayer?  
Imam {a.s} said: 'He should continue with his prayer, for the Lord {AZJ} of the water is the 
Lord {AZJ} of the earth.'  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 21, H. 221

hadiths.chapter: 21. THE CHAPTER ON TAYAMMUM (DRY ABLUTION)
hadiths.ar:
223  -  وَّ سَّا َّلَّ عَّن ْذُّ إَّلز َّجْمَّن ِ ن ْن ُّ إ َّن ِى  ن َّح ْزَّإن َّ إ َّن َّا إَّلْحَّسَّن ِ مُّوسَّى ن ْن َّ ج َّعْق َّزٍ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ : عَّن ْ  ي َّلاَّي َّه ِ ن َّق َّزٍ كَّان ُّوإ ق ِى  سَّق َّزٍ 
إ َّجَّذُّهُّمْ ج ُّن ُّث   وَّ إَّلي  َّان ِى  مَّي ِّث   وَّ إَّلي  َّالِث ُّ عَّلَّى ع َّي ْزِ وُّص ُّوءٍ وَّ جَّص َّزَّب ِ إَّلص َّلاَّة ُّ وَّ مَّعَّهُّمْ مِن َّ إَّلْمَّاءِ  ق َّذْرُّ مَّا ي َّكْق ِى  إ َّجَّذَّهُّمْ 
مَّن ْ ي َّا ْج ُّذ ُّ إَّلْمَّاءَّ وَّ كَّي ْف َّ ي َّصْن َّعُّون َّ ق َّق َّال لُّ إَّلْح ُّن ُّث ُّ وَّ ي ُّذْق َّن ُّ إَّلْمَّي ِّث ُّ ن ِي َّي َّم ُّمٍ وَّ ي َّي َّي َّم َّمُّ إَّل َّذ ِى  هُّوَّ عَّلَّى ع َّي ْزِ وُّص ُّوءٍ  
لِأ َّن  َّ إَّلْع ُّسْلَّ مِن َّ إَّلْح َّن َّان َّه ِ ق َّزِي ص َّه   وَّ ع ُّسْلَّ إَّلْمَّي ِّث ِ سُّن  َّه   وَّ إَّلي  َّي َّم ُّمَّ لِلْا ج َّزِ ج َّان ِز   .
hadiths.en:
Abu Dharr  came to the Prophet, and said: "O’ Messenger of Allah {saws}, I have 
perished; I had relations (with a women) without water (for ghusl afterwards)."  
The Prophet then ordered a canopy to be used for covering (privacy) and water for 
bathing.  
Abu Dharr said: 'We both (he and his wife) then performed ghusl.  
Then He {saws} said: "'O’ Abu Dharr, clean earth (tayammum) is sufficient for you for ten 
years.' 
[AL SADUQ] 
If a man becomes junub (ritually impure due to sexual discharge) while traveling and 
has only enough water for wudu (ablution), he should perform tayammum instead of 
wudu unless he knows that he will find water before the prayer time expires.  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 1 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, Part 1, p. 194 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, 
Part 1, p. 199 • Al-Arba‘ūn Ḥadīthan (Al-Shahīd al-Awwal) Vol. 1, p. 26 • Awaali al-La’aali, Part 3, p. 47 • Al-
Wafi, Vol. 6, p. 551 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, vol. 3, p. 369 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 78, p. 168 • Mustadrak Al-Wasail, 
Vol. 2, p. 541 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 21, H. 222

hadiths.chapter: 21. THE CHAPTER ON TAYAMMUM (DRY ABLUTION)
hadiths.ar:
224  -  وَّ سَّا َّلَّ مُّحَّم َّذُّ ن ْن ُّ جُّمْزَّإن َّ إَّلن  َّهْذِى  ُّ وَّ ج َّمِي لُّ ن ْن ُّ ذَّر َّإح ٍ إ َّن َّا عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم :  عَّن ْ إ ِمَّامِ ق َّوْمٍ إ َّصَّان َّي ْهُّ ج َّن َّان َّه    
ق ِى  إَّلس َّق َّزِ وَّ لَّي ْسَّ مَّعَّهُّ مِن َّ إَّلْمَّاءِ مَّا ي َّكْق ِي هِ لِلْع ُّسْلِ إ َّ ي َّي َّوَّص  َّا ُّ ن َّعْص ُّهُّمْ وَّ ي ُّصَّلِّى  ن ِهِمْ ق َّق َّالَّ  «
ي ُّصَّلِّى  ن ِهِمْ ق َّا ِن  َّ إَّلل َّهَّ عَّز  َّ وَّ ج َّل  ج َّعَّلَّ إَّلي  ُّزَّإب َّ طَّهُّورإً كَّمَّا ج َّعَّلَّ إَّلْمَّاءَّ طَّهُّورإً .
hadiths.en:
Abdul Rahman ibn Abi Najran asked Abu Al -Hasan Imam Musa ibn Jafar Al -
Kadhim {a.s} about three people who were traveling: one was junub (in a state of major 
ritual impurity), the second was dead, and the third was without wudu (ablution). The 
time for prayer came, and they had only enough water for one of them. Who should 
use the water and what should they do?  
Imam {a.s} said: 'The one who is junub should perform ghusl (ritual bathing). The 
deceased should be buried with tayammum, and the one without wudu should 
perform tayammum because ghusl from janabah is a religious obligation, the washing 
of the deceased is a recomme nded practice, and tayammum for the other is 
permissible.'  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Awaali al-La’aali, Part 3, p. 49 • Wasa’il Al -Shi’a, vol. 3, 
p. 375 • Bihar Al Anwar, Vol. 78, p. 26 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 21, H. 223

hadiths.chapter: 21. THE CHAPTER ON TAYAMMUM (DRY ABLUTION)
hadiths.ar:
225   -  وَّ سَّا َّلَّ عَّن ْذُّ إَّلل َّهِ ن ْن سِن َّان ٍ إ َّن َّا عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ :   عَّن ِ إَّلز َّج ُّلِ ي ُّصِي ن ُّهُّ إَّلْح َّن َّان َّه ُّ ق ِى  إَّلل َّي ْلَّه ِ إَّلْن َّارِذَّة ِ وَّ ي َّح َّاف ُّ  
عَّلَّى ن َّق ْسِهِ إَّلي  َّلَّف َّ إ ِن ِ إِع ْي َّسَّلَّ ق َّق َّال ذَّ إَّلص َّلاَّة َّ .   
وإ ذ إ كان  إلزج ل ق ى  جال لأ ي ق ذر إ لأ على إلطي ن  ي ي ي مم ن ه ق ا ن  إلله ي ن ارك  وي عالى إ ولى ن العذ ر إ ذ إ لم ي كن  معه ي وب  ج اف  ولأ  
لن ذ ي ق ذر على إ ن  ي ن ق ص ه و ي ي ي مم من ه .  ومن  كان  ق ى  وسظ ر جام ي وم إلح معه  إ و ي وم عزق ه  ولم ي سي طغ إلح زوح  من  إلمسح ذ 
من  كي زة  إلن اس ي ي مم وصلى معهم ولي عذ  إ ذ إ إن صزف .  
ومن  ي ي مم وكان  معه ماء ق ن سى  وصلى ن ي ي مم، ي م ذ كز ق ن ل إ ن  ي ح زح  إلوق ث  ق لي عذ إلوص وء وإلصلاة .   ومن  إجي لم ق ى  مسح ذ  
من  إلمساج ذ ج زح  من ه وإع ي سل، إ لأ إ ن  ي كون  إجي لامه ق ى  إلمسح ذ إلحزإم إ و ق ى  مسح ذ إلزسول صلى إلله علي ه وإ له ق ا ن ه  
إ ن  إجي لم ق ى  إ جذ هذ ي ن  إلمسح ذي ن  ي ي مم وج زح  ولم ي مس  ق ي هما إ لأ مي ي مما.
hadiths.en:
Muhammad ibn Hamran al -Nahdi and Jamil ibn Darraj asked Imam Jafar ibn 
Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} about an Imam leading a group in prayer who becomes junub 
(in a state of major ritual impurity) while traveling and does not have enough water for 
ghusl (ritual bathing). Should some of them perform wudu and then lead the prayer in 
his place?  
Imam {a.s} said: 'No, rather the junub should perform tayammum and lead them in 
prayer, for Allah {SWT}, the Mighty and Majestic, has made the earth (dust) a means of 
purification just as He has made water a means of purification.'  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Al -Kafi, Volume 3, p. 66 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, Part 3, p. 
167 • Al-Istibsar, Part 1, p. 425 • Al -Wafi, Vol. 6, p. 543 • Wasa’il Al -Shi’a, vol. 3, p. 386 • Wasa’il Al -Shi’a, 
vol. 8, p. 327 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 21, H. 224

hadiths.chapter: 21. THE CHAPTER ON TAYAMMUM (DRY ABLUTION)
hadiths.en:
Abdullah ibn Sinan asked Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} about a man 
who becomes junub (ritually impure) during a cold night and fears for his life if he 
performs ghusl (ritual bathing).  
Imam {a.s} said: 'He should perform tayammum and pray, and when he is safe from the 
cold, he should perform ghusl and repeat the prayer.'  
[AL SADUQ] 
If a person is in a situation where they only have access to mud, they should perform 
tayammum with it, for Allah {SWT}, the Blessed and Exalted, is more deserving of granting 
an excuse if they do not have dry clothing or anything they can shake off and use for 
tayammum. If a person finds themselves in a crowded place on the day of Jumu'ah 
(Jumu’ah prayer) or on the Day of  Arafah and cannot leave the mosque due to the 
crowd, they should perform tayammum, pray with the congregation, and then repeat 
(the prayer) once they have left. 
If a person performs tayammum but had water available and forgot, and prayed using 
tayammum, then remembered before the prayer time ended, they should perform 
wudu and repeat the prayer. If someone experiences a nocturnal emission (becomes 
junub) while in a mosque, they should leave and perform ghusl, except if it occurs in 
the Sacred Mosque (Masjid al -Haram) or the Mosque of the Messenger {saws}. In these 
two mosques, they should perform tayammum and leave without walking in them 
except while in a state of tayammum. 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, vol. 3, p. 366 • Wasa’il Al -Shi’a, vol. 3, 
p. 372

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 21, H. 225

hadiths.chapter: 22. THE RITUAL BATH OF JUMU’AH, ENTERING THE
hadiths.ar:
227  -  ق َّق َّذْ رَّوَّى إَّلْحَّسَّن ُّ ن ْن ُّ مُّوسَّى ن ْن ِ ج َّعْق َّزٍ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ  عَّن ْ إ ُّمِّهِ وَّ إ ُّمِّ إ َّجْمَّذَّ ن ْن ِ مُّوسَّى عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّالَّي َّا: كُّن  َّا  
مَّغَّ إ َّن ِى  إَّلْحَّسَّن ِ مُّوسَّى ن ْن ِ ج َّعْق َّزٍ  عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ  ق ِى  إَّلْن َّاذِي َّه ِ وَّ ن َّحْن ُّ ن ُّزِي ذُّ ن َّع ْذَّإذَّ ق َّق َّالَّ لَّن َّا ي َّوْمَّ إَّلْح َّمِي سِ 
إَّلْي َّوْمَّ لِع َّذٍ ي َّوْمِ إَّلْح ُّمُّعَّه ِ ق َّا ِن  َّ إَّلْمَّاءَّ ع َّذإً ن ِهَّا ق َّلِي ل مُّعَّه ِ .  
وع سل ي وم إلح معه  سن ه  وإج ن ه ، وي ح ور  من 
إلز وإل، ومن  ن سى  إلع سل إ و ق اي ه لعله  ق لي ع ي سل ن عذ إلعصز إ و ي وم إلسن ث ، وي ح ز ى  إلع سل للح معه  كما ي كون  للزوإح . 
وإلوص وء ق ي ه ق ن ل إلع سل، وي ق ول إلمع ي سل للح معه : " إللهم طهزن ى  وطهز ق لن ى  وإ ن ق  ع سلى  وإ ج ز على لسان ى  محن ه  من ك  .
hadiths.en:
The Messenger of Allah {saws} said:  
‘Whoever believes in Allah {SWT} and the Last Day should not enter the bathhouse except 
with a waistcloth (covering).’  
And Prophet {saws} prohibited washing under the open sky without a covering and 
prohibited entering rivers without a covering, saying:  
'The water has inhabitants and dwellers.' 
[AL SADUQ] 
The Jumu’ah (ghusl) is obligatory for both men and women, whether at home or while 
traveling, though women are granted a pardon when traveling due to scarcity of water. 
If a person is traveling and finds water on Thursday but fears they will not find it on 
Jumu’ah, it is permissible to perform the ghusl for Jumu’ah on Thursday.  
If they find water on Jumu’ah, they should perform the ghusl then, but if they do not, 
the previous ghusl suffices. 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 22, H. 226

hadiths.chapter: 22. THE RITUAL BATH OF JUMU’AH, ENTERING THE BATHHOUSE, ITS ETIQUETTE, AND TEACHINGS ON
hadiths.ar:
228  -  وَّق َّال إَّلص َّاذِق ُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ :  «-  إ َّس ْهَّذُّ إ َّن ْ لأَّ إ ِلَّهَّ إ ِلأ َّ إَّلل َّهُّ وَّجْذَّةُّ لأَّ س َّزِي ك َّ لَّهُّ وَّ إ َّن 
مُّحَّم َّذإً عَّن ْذُّةُّ وَّ رَّسُّولُّهُّ إَّلل َّهُّم َّ صَّلِّ عَّلَّى مُّحَّم َّذٍ وَّ إ لِ مُّحَّم َّذٍ وَّ إِج ْعَّلْن ِى  مِن َّ إَّلي  َّو َّإن ِي ن َّ وَّ إِ ج ْعَّلْن ِى  مِن َّ إَّلْمُّي َّطَّهِّزِي ن َّ كَّان َّ 
طُّهْزإً مِن َّ إَّ لْح ُّمُّعَّه ِ إ ِلَّى إَّلْح ُّمُّعَّه ِ .
hadiths.en:
Al-Hasan ibn Musa ibn Jafar narrated from his mother and Umm Ahmad bint 
Musa {a.s} who said:  
"We were both with Imam Musa ibn Jafar Al -Kadhim {a.s} in the desert while we were 
heading to Baghdad, and on Thursday He {a.s} said to us,  
'Perform ghusl today for tomorrow, Jumu’ah, because water will be scarce tomorrow.'  
They said: So we performed ghusl on Thursday for Jumu’ah.  
[AL SADUQ] 
The Jumu’ah bath (ghusl) is an emphasized recommended practice, and it can be 
performed from the time of dawn (Fajr) on Jumu’ah until near the time of noon. The 
best time being the most preferred time being close to noon. If one forgets or misses 
the ghusl  due to an excuse, they may perform it after the afternoon or even on 
Saturday, and it will still suffice as the Jumu’ah bath as it serves the purpose of 
purification and preparation. 
Before performing ghusl, one should perform wudu (ablution), and when performing 
the Jumu’ah bath, the person should say: 'O’ Allah {SWT}, purify me, purify my heart, 
cleanse my body, and let love for You flow on my tongue.'  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 22, H. 227

hadiths.chapter: 22. THE RITUAL BATH OF JUMU’AH, ENTERING THE BATHHOUSE, ITS ETIQUETTE, AND TEACHINGS ON
hadiths.en:
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} said:  
"Whoever performs the ghusl (ritual bath) for Jumu’ah and then says:  
"I bear witness that there is no god but Allah {SWT}, alone without any partner, and that 
Muhammad is His servant and Messenger. O’ Allah {SWT}, bless Muhammad and the 
family of Muhammad, and make me among those who turn in repentance and make 
me among those who purify themselves,"  
It will serve as purification for them from (this) Jumu’ah to (next) Jumu’ah.'  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Part 3, p. 10 • Jamal al-Usbu, Part 1, 
p. 368 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 6, p. 513 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, vol. 3, p. 323 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 86, p. 352

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 22, H. 228

hadiths.chapter: 22. THE RITUAL BATH OF JUMU’AH, ENTERING THE BATHHOUSE, ITS ETIQUETTE, AND TEACHINGS ON
hadiths.ar:
230   -  وَّق َّال إَّلص َّاذِق ُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ :  ق ِى  عِل َّه ِ ع ُّسْلِ ي َّوْمِ إَّلْح ُّمُّعَّه ِ 
ق َّا ِذ َّإ كَّان َّ ي َّوْمُّ إَّلْح ُّمُّعَّه ِ جَّص َّزُّوإ إَّلْمَّسْح ِذَّ ق َّي َّا َّذ  َّى إَّلن  َّاسُّ ن ِا َّرْوَّإحِ إ ن َّاطِهِمْ وَّ إ َّج ْسَّاذِهِمْ ق َّا َّمَّزَّ هُّمْ رَّسُّولُّ إَّلل َّهِ  صَّل َّى إَّلل َّهُّ 
عَّلَّي ْهِ وَّ إ لِهِ ن ِالْع ُّسْلِ ق َّح َّزَّب ْ ن ِذ َّلِك َّ إَّلس ُّن  َّه ُّ .
hadiths.en:
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} said:  
'The ghusl (ritual bath) of Jumu’ah is a purification and an expiation for the sins 
committed between one Jumu’ah and the next (Jumu’ah).'  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 6, p. 395 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, vol. 3, p. 315 • 
Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 78, p. 128 • Mustadrak Al-Wasail, Vol. 2, p. 501 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 22, H. 229

hadiths.chapter: 22. THE RITUAL BATH OF JUMU’AH, ENTERING THE BATHHOUSE, ITS ETIQUETTE, AND TEACHINGS ON
hadiths.ar:
231  -  وَّ رُّوِى وي َّعَّالَّى  إ َّي َّم َّ صَّلاَّة َّ إَّلْق َّزِي ص َّه ِ ن ِصَّلاَّة ِ  إَّلن  َّاق ِلَّه ِ وَّ إ َّي َّم َّ صِي َّامَّ إَّلْق َّزِي ص َّه ِ ن ِصِي َّامِ إَّلن  َّاق ِلَّه ِ وَّ 
إ َّي َّم َّ إَّلْوُّص ُّوءَّ ن ِع ُّسْلِ ي َّوْمِ إَّلْح ُّمُّعَّه ِ .
hadiths.en:
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al -Sadiq {a.s} said regarding the reason for the 
Jumu’ah (ritual bath):  
'The Ansar (residents of Medina who supported the Prophet) used to work in their fields 
and with their livestock. When Jumu’ah came, they would attend the mosque, and the 
people were bothered by the odor from their armpits and bodies. So the Messenger of 
Allah {saws} ordered them to perform ghusl, and thus it became a Sunnah (established 
practice).' 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 6, p. 395 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, vol. 3, p. 315 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 22, H. 230

hadiths.chapter: 22. THE RITUAL BATH OF JUMU’AH, ENTERING THE BATHHOUSE, ITS ETIQUETTE, AND TEACHINGS ON
hadiths.en:
(Al-Hussain ibn Khalid asked Abu Al-Hasan Al-Awwal {a.s}) narrates that:  
‘It is narrated that 'Allah {SWT}, the Blessed and Exalted, completes the obligatory prayer 
with the supererogatory prayer, completes the obligatory fasting with the 
supererogatory fasting, and completes the wudu (ablution) with the ghusl (ritual bath) 
of Jumu’ah.’.  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, vol. 3, p. 315

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 22, H. 231

hadiths.chapter: 22. THE RITUAL BATH OF JUMU’AH, ENTERING THE BATHHOUSE, ITS ETIQUETTE, AND TEACHINGS ON
hadiths.en:
Yahya ibn Sa'id Al-Ahwazi narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad ibn Abi Nasr, 
and him from Muhammad ibn Hamran who reported that Imam Al -Sadiq {a.s} said:  
When you enter the bathhouse (hammam), at the time you remove your clothes, say:  
'O’ Allah {SWT}, remove from me shackles of hypocrisy and establish me firmly on faith.'  
And when you enter the first room (of bathhouse), say: 'O’ Allah {SWT}, I seek refuge with 
You from the evil of my soul and seek protection from its harm.'"  
And when you enter the second room (of bathhouse), say: 'O’ Allah {SWT}, remove 
impurity and defilement from me, and purify my body and heart.'  
Take some warm water and pour it over your head and pour it over your feet. If you can 
drink a sip of it, then do so, as it cleanses the bladder. Remain in second room for a 
while.  
When you enter the third room (of bathhouse), say:  
'We seek refuge in Allah {SWT} from the Fire and ask Him for Paradise,' and repeat it until 
the time you leave the hot room (of bathhouse).  
Be careful not to drink cold water or consuming ‘fuqqa'a’ (like carbonated drinks) in 
the bathhouse, as it harms the stomach.  
Do not pour cold water over yourself, as it weakens the body. However, pouring cold 
water on your feet when leaving will drive away illness from your body.  
When you put on your clothes, say: 'O’ Allah {SWT}, clothe me with piety and protect me 
from ruin.' If you do this, you will be safe from all ailments.'  
[AL SADUQ] 
There is no harm in reciting the Qur'an in the bathhouse as long as you do not raise 
your voice and you are wearing a waistcloth. 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Al-Amali (by Al-Saduq) Vol. 1, p. 363 • Al-Waezin, volume 
2, page 307 • Makarim Al-Akhlaq, vol. 1, p. 52 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 6, p. 601 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, vol. 2, p. 44 • Bihar 
Al Anwar, Vol. 73, p. 70 • Bihar Al Anwar, Vol. 73, p. 76

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 22, H. 232

hadiths.chapter: 22. THE RITUAL BATH OF JUMU’AH, ENTERING THE BATHHOUSE, ITS ETIQUETTE, AND TEACHINGS ON
hadiths.ar:
234 - وَّق َّال عَّلِى  ُّ ن ْن ُّ ي َّق ْطِي ن ٍ لِمُّوسَّى ن ْن ِ ج َّعْق َّزٍ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ : إ َّق ْزَّإ ُّ ق ِى  إَّلْحَّم َّامِ وَّ إ َّن ْكِحُّ ق ِي هِ ق َّال .    
وي ح ث  على إلزج ل إ ن  ي ع ض  ن صزة وي سي ز ق زج ه من  إ ن  ي ن ط ز إ لي ه.
hadiths.en:
Muhammad ibn Muslim asked Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al -Baqir {a.s} saying:  
'Did Commander of the Faithful {a.s} prohibit recitation of the Qur'an in the bathhouse?' 
Imam {a.s} said: 'No, he only prohibited a man from reciting while being naked. If he is 
wearing a waistcloth (covering), then there is no harm.'  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Makarim Al-Akhlaq, vol. 1, p. 52 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 22, H. 233

hadiths.chapter: 22. THE RITUAL BATH OF JUMU’AH, ENTERING THE BATHHOUSE, ITS ETIQUETTE, AND TEACHINGS ON
hadiths.ar:
235 - وَّ سُّن ِلَّ إَّلص َّاذِق ُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم : عَّن ْ ق َّوْلِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّز  َّ وَّ ج َّل ۝ ق ُّلْ لِلْمُّو ْمِن ِي ن َّ ي َّع ُّص  ُّوإ مِن ْ إ َّن ْصٰارِهِمْ وَّ ي َّحْق َّط ُّوإ 
ق ُّزُّوج َّهُّمْ ذ ٰلِك َّ إ َّر ْكىٰ لَّهُّمْ   ۝»  ق َّق َّال 
هَّذ َّإ إَّلْمَّوْص ِغِ ق َّا ِن  َّهُّ لِلْحِق ْظ ِ مِن ْ إ َّن ْ ي ُّن ْط َّزَّ إ ِلَّي ْهِ .
hadiths.en:
Ali ibn Yaqtin said to Imam Musa ibn Jafar Al-Kadhim {a.s}:  
'May I recite (in the bathhouse) and marry (perform a marriage contract) there?'  
Imam {a.s} said: 'There is no harm.' 
[AL SADUQ] 
A man must lower his gaze and cover his private parts to prevent others from looking 
at them (in bathhouse). 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Makarim Al-Akhlaq, V.1, p. 53 • Bihar Al Anwar, V.73, p. 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 22, H. 234

hadiths.chapter: 22. THE RITUAL BATH OF JUMU’AH, ENTERING THE BATHHOUSE, ITS ETIQUETTE, AND TEACHINGS ON
hadiths.en:
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al -Sadiq {a.s} was asked about the saying of Allah 
{SWT}, the Exalted: " Tell the believing men to lower their gaze and guard their private 
parts; that is purer for them." (Surah An-Nur, 24:30). 
Imam {a.s} said: 'Every mention of guarding the private parts in the Book of Allah {SWT}, the 
Exalted, refers to guarding against fornication, except in this instance, where it refers 
to guarding them from being looked at.' 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 6, p. 595 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, vol. 1, p. 300 • 
Al-Fusul al-Muhimmah, Part 1, p. 696

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 22, H. 235

hadiths.chapter: 22. THE RITUAL BATH OF JUMU’AH, ENTERING THE BATHHOUSE, ITS ETIQUETTE, AND TEACHINGS ON
hadiths.en:
On the authority of Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al -Sadiq {a.s} that He said:  
"Indeed, looking at the private parts of a Muslim is disliked, and looking at the private 
parts of a non-Muslim is like looking at the private parts of a donkey."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Makarim Al-Akhlaq, vol. 1, p. 56 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 6, p. 596 
• Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, vol. 2, p. 36 • Bihar Al Anwar, Vol. 73, p. 80 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 101, p. 42 • Mustadrak 
Al-Wasail, Part 1, p. 378 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 22, H. 236

hadiths.chapter: 22. THE RITUAL BATH OF JUMU’AH, ENTERING THE BATHHOUSE, ITS ETIQUETTE, AND TEACHINGS ON
hadiths.en:
The Commander of the Faithful {a.s} said: 'What a good place the bathhouse is, 
where one is reminded of the Fire (Hellfire) and it removes filth.'  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Makarim Al-Akhlaq, v.1, p. 53 • Wasa’il Al-Shia, vol. 2, p. 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 22, H. 237

hadiths.chapter: 22. THE RITUAL BATH OF JUMU’AH, ENTERING THE BATHHOUSE, ITS ETIQUETTE, AND TEACHINGS ON
hadiths.ar:
239 - وَّق َّال إَّلص َّاذِق ُّ عَّ :  « إَّلن  َّارِ.  
ومن  إلأ ذإب : إ ن  لأ ي ذج ل إلزج ل ولذة معه إلحمام ق ي ن ط ز إ لى عوري ه.
hadiths.en:
Imam {a.s} said: 'The bathhouse is an undesirable place; it removes coverings 
(privacy) and takes away modesty.' 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Makarim Al-Akhlaq, vol. 1, p. 53 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 6, p. 591 
• Wasa’il Al-Shia, vol. 2, p. 30 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 22, H. 238

hadiths.chapter: 22. THE RITUAL BATH OF JUMU’AH, ENTERING THE BATHHOUSE, ITS ETIQUETTE, AND TEACHINGS ON
hadiths.en:
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} said: 'The bathhouse is an undesirable 
place; it removes coverings (privacy) and exposes the private parts.  
However, it is a good place as it reminds one of the heats of the Fire (Hellfire).'.  
[AL SADUQ] 
Among the etiquettes: a man should not enter the bathhouse with his child if it would 
lead to the child seeing his private parts.  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 6, p. 591 • Wasa’il Al-Shia, vol. 2, p. 30

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 22, H. 239

hadiths.chapter: 22. THE RITUAL BATH OF JUMU’AH, ENTERING THE BATHHOUSE, ITS ETIQUETTE, AND TEACHINGS ON
hadiths.en:
The Messenger of Allah {saws} said: "Whoever believes in Allah {SWT} and the Last 
Day (Qiyamah) should not send his wife to the bathhouse."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Makarim Al -Akhlaq, vol. 1, p. 53 • Wasa’il Al -Shi’a, vol. 
2, p. 49 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 22, H. 240
